docs.title: info
docs.text:
The curated CLI command reference for developers.

Stop googling the same commands. Bookmark this instead.

docs.title: Sections
docs.text: Section What's inside CLI Power Tools Modern replacements for grep, find, cat, ls, and cd Git Beyond the basics — rebase, bisect, worktrees, gh CLI Docker Build, run, compose, prune — the commands you actually use AI CLI Tools Claude Code, GitHub Copilot CLI, and other AI-assisted workflows Shell & Productivity zsh, starship, tmux, and dotfile essentials Networking & Security curl, ssh, openssl, and debugging network issues Cloud CLI AWS, GCP, Azure, kubectl, and terraform quick reference macOS Power User Homebrew, defaults write, system utilities Package Managers winget, brew, scoop, nix, mise — cross-platform Node.js & JS Toolchain npm, pnpm, bun, npx, tsx, and debugging

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Install the tools that make everything else faster:

macOSLinuxWindows 
brew install ripgrep fd bat eza zoxide fzf jq

sudo apt install ripgrep fd-find bat fzf jq
# eza and zoxide via cargo or binary releases
cargo install eza zoxide

winget install BurntSushi.ripgrep sharkdp.fd sharkdp.bat eza-community.eza ajeetdsouza.zoxide junegunn.fzf jqlang.jq

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Found a better way? Missing a command you use daily? See the contributing guide.

docs.title: AI CLI Tools
docs.text: AI-assisted development from the terminal. Generate commands, explain code, automate tasks.

docs.title: Claude Code
docs.text:
Interactive AI coding agent in the terminal:

claude

Start with a specific task:

claude "refactor auth middleware to use JWT"

Run in non-interactive mode for scripting:

claude -p "explain this error" < error.log

Resume the most recent conversation:

claude --continue

Install: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

docs.title: GitHub Copilot CLI
docs.text:
Get command suggestions from natural language:

gh copilot suggest "find all files larger than 100MB"

Explain a command you're unsure about:

gh copilot explain "tar -xzvf archive.tar.gz"

Install: gh extension install github/gh-copilot

docs.title: llm (Simon Willison)
docs.text:
Send a prompt to any LLM from the terminal:

llm "write a bash function to retry a command 3 times"

Pipe content for analysis:

cat error.log | llm "summarize the errors and suggest fixes"

Use with different models:

llm -m claude-sonnet-4-6 "review this code for security issues" < auth.py

Install: pip install llm · brew install llm

docs.title: Aider
docs.text:
AI pair programming — edits files directly:

aider src/auth.py src/middleware.py

Start with a specific model:

aider --model claude-sonnet-4-6

Install: pip install aider-chat

docs.title: Pipe Patterns
docs.text:
These patterns work with any AI CLI tool:

Pass a diff for review:

git diff | claude -p "review this diff for bugs"

Generate a commit message:

git diff --staged | llm "write a concise commit message for these changes"

Explain a complex command:

echo "find . -name '*.log' -mtime +30 -exec gzip {} \;" | gh copilot explain

docs.title: CLI Power Tools
docs.text: Modern replacements for standard Unix utilities. Faster, more intuitive defaults, and better output.

docs.title: ripgrep — faster grep
docs.text:
Recursively search file contents, respecting .gitignore by default:

rg "TODO" --type py

Search with context (3 lines before and after):

rg -C 3 "function.*auth"

Search only specific file types:

rg "import" --type ts --type tsx

Install: brew install ripgrep · apt install ripgrep · winget install BurntSushi.ripgrep

docs.title: fd — faster find
docs.text:
Find files by name with sensible defaults:

fd "\.json$" src/

Find and delete all .DS_Store files:

fd -H .DS_Store -x rm

Find files modified in the last hour:

fd --changed-within 1h

Install: brew install fd · apt install fd-find · winget install sharkdp.fd

docs.title: bat — better cat
docs.text:
View files with syntax highlighting and line numbers:

bat src/index.ts

Show only a range of lines:

bat --line-range 20:40 config.yml

Use as a man pager:

export MANPAGER="sh -c 'col -bx | bat -l man -p'"

Install: brew install bat · apt install bat · winget install sharkdp.bat

Ubuntu/Debian

The binary is installed as batcat due to a name conflict. Create an alias: alias bat='batcat'

docs.title: eza — modern ls
docs.text:
List files with git status and icons:

eza -la --git --icons

Tree view with depth limit:

eza --tree --level=2

Install: brew install eza · cargo install eza · winget install eza-community.eza

docs.title: zoxide — smarter cd
docs.text:
Jump to a frequently used directory:

z projects

Interactive directory picker (requires fzf):

zi

Add to your shell (put in .zshrc or .bashrc):

eval "$(zoxide init zsh)"

Install: brew install zoxide · cargo install zoxide · winget install ajeetdsouza.zoxide

docs.title: fzf — fuzzy finder
docs.text:
Interactive fuzzy search for anything piped to it:

find . -type f | fzf

Search and open a file in your editor:

vim $(fzf)

Preview files while browsing:

fzf --preview 'bat --color=always {}'

Install: brew install fzf · apt install fzf · winget install junegunn.fzf

docs.title: jq — JSON processor
docs.text:
Pretty-print JSON:

curl -s https://api.example.com/data | jq .

Extract a specific field:

cat data.json | jq '.users[].name'

Filter and transform:

jq '[.items[] | select(.status == "active") | {name, id}]' data.json

Install: brew install jq · apt install jq · winget install jqlang.jq

docs.title: delta — better git diffs
docs.text:
Configure as your default git diff pager:

git config --global core.pager delta
git config --global interactive.diffFilter "delta --color-only"

Side-by-side view:

git diff | delta --side-by-side

Install: brew install git-delta · cargo install git-delta

docs.title: Cloud CLI
docs.text: The commands you actually use across AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes, and Terraform.

docs.title: Identity & Auth
docs.text:
Check who you're authenticated as:

aws sts get-caller-identity

List configured profiles:

aws configure list-profiles

Use a specific profile:

export AWS_PROFILE=production

docs.title: S3
docs.text:
List buckets:

aws s3 ls

Sync a local directory to S3:

aws s3 sync ./dist s3://my-bucket/app --delete

Download a file:

aws s3 cp s3://my-bucket/data.json .

docs.title: CloudWatch Logs
docs.text:
Tail logs in real time:

aws logs tail /aws/lambda/my-function --follow

Install: brew install awscli · pip install awscli

docs.title: Auth & Config
docs.text:
Login interactively:

gcloud auth login

Set the active project:

gcloud config set project my-project-id

List active configuration:

gcloud config list

docs.title: Compute
docs.text:
SSH into a VM:

gcloud compute ssh my-instance --zone us-central1-a

docs.title: Cloud Run
docs.text:
Deploy a container:

gcloud run deploy my-service --source . --region us-central1

Install: brew install google-cloud-sdk

docs.title: Azure (az)
docs.text:
Login:

az login

List subscriptions:

az account list --output table

Set active subscription:

az account set --subscription "My Subscription"

Install: brew install azure-cli

docs.title: Context & Namespace
docs.text:
List contexts:

kubectl config get-contexts

Switch context:

kubectl config use-context production

Set default namespace:

kubectl config set-context --current --namespace=my-app

docs.title: Pods
docs.text:
List pods:

kubectl get pods

View logs:

kubectl logs -f deployment/my-app

Exec into a pod:

kubectl exec -it my-pod -- /bin/sh

Port-forward a service to localhost:

kubectl port-forward svc/my-service 8080:80

docs.title: Quick Debug
docs.text:
Get events sorted by time:

kubectl get events --sort-by=.lastTimestamp

Describe a failing pod:

kubectl describe pod my-pod

Install: brew install kubectl · comes with Docker Desktop

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
Initialize a project:

terraform init

Preview changes:

terraform plan

Apply changes:

terraform apply

Destroy all managed resources:

terraform destroy

Warning

Always run terraform plan before apply in production environments.

docs.title: State
docs.text:
List managed resources:

terraform state list

Show a specific resource:

terraform state show aws_instance.web

Install: brew install terraform · winget install Hashicorp.Terraform

docs.title: Docker
docs.text: Build, run, ship. The commands you reach for daily.

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
Build an image from the current directory:

docker build -t myapp:latest .

Build with a specific Dockerfile and build arg:

docker build -f Dockerfile.prod --build-arg NODE_ENV=production -t myapp:prod .

docs.title: Run
docs.text:
Run a container interactively with port mapping:

docker run -it -p 3000:3000 myapp:latest

Run detached with a name and auto-remove on exit:

docker run -d --rm --name myapp -p 8080:8080 myapp:latest

Run with a volume mount (bind mount for development):

docker run -v $(pwd)/src:/app/src -p 3000:3000 myapp:latest

docs.title: Exec
docs.text:
Open a shell in a running container:

docker exec -it myapp /bin/sh

Run a one-off command:

docker exec myapp cat /app/config.json

docs.title: Compose
docs.text:
Start all services defined in docker-compose.yml:

docker compose up -d

Rebuild and start:

docker compose up -d --build

View logs for a specific service:

docker compose logs -f api

Stop and remove everything (containers, networks):

docker compose down

Stop and remove everything including volumes:

docker compose down -v

docs.title: Inspect & Debug
docs.text:
List running containers:

docker ps

List all containers (including stopped):

docker ps -a

View container logs:

docker logs -f --tail 100 myapp

Inspect a container's network, mounts, config:

docker inspect myapp

docs.title: Cleanup
docs.text:
Remove all stopped containers:

docker container prune

Remove all unused images:

docker image prune -a

Nuclear option — reclaim all unused space:

docker system prune -a --volumes

Warning

docker system prune -a --volumes removes all unused images, containers, networks, and volumes. Make sure you don't need any of them.

Show disk usage:

docker system df

docs.title: Podman (Rootless Alternative)
docs.text:
Most Docker commands work identically with podman:

podman build -t myapp .
podman run -it -p 3000:3000 myapp
podman compose up -d

Install: brew install podman · apt install podman · winget install RedHat.Podman

docs.title: Git
docs.text: Beyond add, commit, push. The commands that save you when things go sideways.

docs.title: Interactive Rebase
docs.text:
Rewrite the last 5 commits (reorder, squash, edit messages):

git rebase -i HEAD~5

Warning

Never rebase commits that have been pushed to a shared branch.

docs.title: Bisect
docs.text:
Find the commit that introduced a bug using binary search:

git bisect start
git bisect bad                  # current commit is broken
git bisect good v1.2.0          # this tag was working
# Git checks out a middle commit — test it, then:
git bisect good                 # or: git bisect bad
# Repeat until Git identifies the first bad commit
git bisect reset                # return to original HEAD

docs.title: Worktrees
docs.text:
Work on multiple branches simultaneously without stashing:

git worktree add ../feature-branch feature-branch

List active worktrees:

git worktree list

Remove when done:

git worktree remove ../feature-branch

docs.title: Reflog — Undo Almost Anything
docs.text:
See every HEAD movement (even after reset):

git reflog

Recover a commit you accidentally reset away:

git reset --hard HEAD@{3}

docs.title: Stash
docs.text:
Stash with a descriptive name:

git stash push -m "WIP: auth refactor"

List stashes:

git stash list

Apply a specific stash without removing it:

git stash apply stash@{2}

docs.title: GitHub CLI (gh)
docs.text:
Create a PR from the current branch:

gh pr create --fill

Check out a PR locally by number:

gh pr checkout 42

View CI status for the current branch:

gh pr checks

Browse the repo in your browser:

gh repo view --web

Search issues:

gh issue list --label "bug" --state open

Install: brew install gh · winget install GitHub.cli

docs.title: Useful Aliases
docs.text:
Add to ~/.gitconfig under [alias]:

[alias]
    lg = log --oneline --graph --all --decorate
    co = checkout
    br = branch -vv
    st = status -sb
    undo = reset --soft HEAD~1
    amend = commit --amend --no-edit
    wip = !git add -A && git commit -m "WIP"

docs.title: Log Tricks
docs.text:
Commits by a specific author in the last week:

git log --author="jason" --since="1 week ago" --oneline

Show files changed in each commit:

git log --stat --oneline -10

Search commit messages:

git log --grep="fix auth" --oneline

docs.title: macOS Power User
docs.text: System utilities, Homebrew management, and hidden settings.

docs.title: Keyboard Symbols
docs.text: Symbol Key Cmd Command Option Option Ctrl Control Shift Shift

docs.title: Bundle — Reproducible Setup
docs.text:
Export your installed packages to a Brewfile:

brew bundle dump --file=~/Brewfile

Install everything from a Brewfile:

brew bundle --file=~/Brewfile

docs.title: Maintenance
docs.text:
Update Homebrew and all packages:

brew update && brew upgrade

Clean up old versions:

brew cleanup --prune=all

List packages not depended on by anything:

brew leaves

docs.title: mas — Mac App Store CLI
docs.text:
List installed App Store apps:

mas list

Search and install:

mas search "Xcode"
mas install 497799835

Install: brew install mas

docs.title: caffeinate — Prevent Sleep
docs.text:
Keep the system awake for 2 hours:

caffeinate -t 7200

Keep awake while a command runs:

caffeinate -s ./long-running-script.sh

docs.title: Software Updates
docs.text:
Check for and install all updates:

softwareupdate --list
softwareupdate --install --all

docs.title: Disk Utilities
docs.text:
List all disks:

diskutil list

Get SMART status:

diskutil info disk0 | grep SMART

docs.title: defaults write — Hidden Settings
docs.text:
Show all file extensions in Finder:

defaults write NSGlobalDomain AppleShowAllExtensions -bool true

Show hidden files in Finder:

defaults write com.apple.finder AppleShowAllFiles -bool true

Disable the "Are you sure you want to open this application?" dialog:

defaults write com.apple.LaunchServices LSQuarantine -bool false

Speed up Mission Control animations:

defaults write com.apple.dock expose-animation-duration -float 0.1

Speed up SMB network share browsing (skip .DS_Store reads):

defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE

Re-enable default behavior:

defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool FALSE

Restart affected services after changes:

killall Finder Dock

Tip

Read current values first with defaults read <domain> <key> before changing them.

docs.title: Clipboard
docs.text:
Copy a file's contents to clipboard:

pbcopy < ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

Paste clipboard contents to a file:

pbpaste > output.txt

docs.title: Finder Navigation
docs.text: Shortcut Action Shift+Cmd+. Toggle hidden files Shift+Cmd+G Go to folder Cmd+Shift+A Applications folder Cmd+Shift+U Utilities folder

docs.title: Quarantine Log
docs.text:
View all downloaded files macOS has tracked:

sqlite3 ~/Library/Preferences/com.apple.LaunchServices.QuarantineEventsV* \
  'select LSQuarantineDataURLString from LSQuarantineEvent'

Clear the log:

sqlite3 ~/Library/Preferences/com.apple.LaunchServices.QuarantineEventsV* \
  'delete from LSQuarantineEvent'

docs.title: Networking & Security
docs.text: Debug APIs, manage SSH, inspect certificates, probe networks.

docs.title: curl — Modern Patterns
docs.text:
POST JSON (the --json flag sets Content-Type and method automatically):

curl --json '{"name":"test"}' https://api.example.com/users

Measure request timing:

curl -o /dev/null -s -w "DNS: %{time_namelookup}s\nConnect: %{time_connect}s\nTLS: %{time_appconnect}s\nTotal: %{time_total}s\n" https://example.com

Follow redirects and show response headers:

curl -sSL -D - https://example.com -o /dev/null

Download with retry and resume:

curl -LC - --retry 3 -o file.zip https://example.com/file.zip

docs.title: httpie — Human-Friendly HTTP
docs.text:
GET request with automatic formatting:

http https://api.example.com/users

POST with JSON body:

http POST https://api.example.com/users name=test email=test@example.com

Send with a bearer token:

http https://api.example.com/me Authorization:"Bearer $TOKEN"

Install: brew install httpie · pip install httpie

docs.title: Config File (~/.ssh/config)
docs.text:
Host myserver
    HostName 192.168.1.100
    User deploy
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
    Port 22

Host jump
    HostName bastion.example.com
    User admin

Host internal
    HostName 10.0.0.5
    ProxyJump jump

docs.title: Generate an Ed25519 key:
docs.text: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "you@example.com"

docs.title: Start the agent and add your key:
docs.text:
eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

docs.title: Copy public key to a server:
docs.text: ssh-copy-id myserver

docs.title: Port forwarding (local):
docs.text:
Access remote port 5432 at localhost:5432:

ssh -L 5432:localhost:5432 myserver

docs.title: Tunnel through a jump host:
docs.text: ssh -J jump internal

docs.title: OpenSSL — Certificate Inspection
docs.text:
View a remote server's TLS certificate:

echo | openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 -servername example.com 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -text

Check certificate expiration:

echo | openssl s_client -connect example.com:443 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates

Inspect a local certificate file:

openssl x509 -in cert.pem -noout -text

Verify a certificate chain:

openssl verify -CAfile ca-bundle.crt cert.pem

docs.title: DNS
docs.text:
Lookup DNS records with dig:

dig example.com A +short
dig example.com MX +short
dig example.com TXT +short

Trace DNS resolution path:

dig +trace example.com

docs.title: dog (modern DNS client)
docs.text:
dog example.com A AAAA MX

Install: brew install dog

docs.title: Network Debugging
docs.text:
Which process is using a port:

macOSLinux 
lsof -i :3000

ss -tlnp | grep :3000

Test TCP connectivity:

nc -zv example.com 443

Scan open ports on a host:

nmap -sT -p 1-1000 192.168.1.1

Note

Only scan hosts you own or have explicit permission to test.

docs.title: Node.js & JS Toolchain
docs.text: Package managers, runners, debugging, and TypeScript workflows.

docs.title: npm
docs.text:
Install dependencies:

npm install

Add a dev dependency:

npm install -D vitest

Run a script from package.json:

npm run build

Check for outdated packages:

npm outdated

Update interactively:

npx npm-check-updates -i

docs.title: pnpm
docs.text:
Drop-in npm replacement with faster installs and disk savings:

pnpm install
pnpm add -D typescript
pnpm run dev

Install: brew install pnpm · npm install -g pnpm

docs.title: Bun
docs.text:
All-in-one runtime, bundler, and package manager:

bun install             # install deps (reads package.json)
bun run dev             # run scripts
bun add hono            # add a dependency

Run a file directly (no build step):

bun run server.ts

Install: brew install oven-sh/bun/bun · curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash

docs.title: npx — Run Without Installing
docs.text:
Run a package without installing globally:

npx create-next-app@latest my-app

Use a specific version:

npx -p typescript@5 tsc --init

docs.title: tsx — Run TypeScript Directly
docs.text:
Execute TypeScript without a build step (via Node.js):

tsx src/script.ts

Watch mode:

tsx watch src/server.ts

Install: npm install -g tsx

docs.title: Node.js Inspector
docs.text:
Start with the debugger:

node --inspect src/index.js

Break on first line:

node --inspect-brk src/index.js

Then open chrome://inspect in Chrome to connect.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Inline environment variables:

NODE_ENV=production node src/index.js

Load from .env file (Node.js 20.6+):

node --env-file=.env src/index.js

docs.title: Vitest / Jest CLI
docs.text:
Run tests:

npx vitest run

Watch mode:

npx vitest

Run a specific test file:

npx vitest run src/auth.test.ts

Run tests matching a pattern:

npx jest --testPathPattern="auth"

Show coverage:

npx vitest run --coverage

docs.title: Comparison
docs.text: Feature npm pnpm Bun Speed Baseline ~2x faster ~5x faster Disk usage Duplicates deps Hard links (saves space) Compact Lockfile package-lock.json pnpm-lock.yaml bun.lockb Built-in runner No No Yes (TypeScript, JSX) Compatibility Universal High Growing

docs.title: Package Managers
docs.text: Install, update, and manage software across platforms.

docs.title: Homebrew (macOS / Linux)
docs.text:
Install Homebrew:

/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

Search for a package:

brew search node

Install a CLI tool vs. a GUI app:

brew install node          # CLI formula
brew install --cask firefox  # GUI cask

See what's outdated:

brew outdated

Upgrade everything:

brew upgrade

docs.title: winget (Windows)
docs.text:
Search for a package:

winget search "Visual Studio Code"

Install:

winget install Microsoft.VisualStudioCode

Upgrade all installed packages:

winget upgrade --all

Export installed packages:

winget export -o packages.json

Import on a new machine:

winget import -i packages.json

Built into Windows 11. Windows 10 users: install via Microsoft Store.

docs.title: Scoop (Windows)
docs.text:
Installs to user directory — no admin required:

irm get.scoop.sh | iex

Add the extras bucket for more packages:

scoop bucket add extras

Install and manage:

scoop install git nodejs python
scoop update *

Best for dev tools. Use winget for GUI apps.

docs.title: Nix (Cross-Platform)
docs.text:
Install Nix:

sh <(curl -L https://nixos.org/nix/install)

Run a tool without installing it:

nix run nixpkgs#cowsay -- "hello"

Enter a shell with specific tools available:

nix shell nixpkgs#nodejs nixpkgs#python3

Tip

Nix guarantees reproducible environments. Great for CI and shared dev setups.

docs.title: mise (Runtime Version Manager)
docs.text:
Replaces nvm, pyenv, rbenv, goenv with a single tool:

mise use node@22
mise use python@3.12

Install a specific version:

mise install node@22.0.0

List installed runtimes:

mise ls

Set a global default:

mise use --global node@22

Project-local versions (creates .mise.toml):

mise use node@20

Install: brew install mise · cargo install mise · curl https://mise.run | sh

docs.title: Comparison
docs.text: Feature Homebrew winget Scoop Nix mise Platform macOS, Linux Windows Windows Cross-platform Cross-platform Best for General packages GUI + CLI apps Dev tools (no admin) Reproducible envs Runtime versions Admin required No Sometimes No No No

docs.title: Shell & Productivity
docs.text: Terminal setup, shell customization, and session management.

docs.title: Starship Prompt
docs.text:
Minimal, fast, cross-shell prompt with git integration:

eval "$(starship init zsh)"

Create a config file:

mkdir -p ~/.config && starship preset pure-preset -o ~/.config/starship.toml

Install: brew install starship · cargo install starship · winget install Starship.Starship

docs.title: zsh-autosuggestions
docs.text:
Fish-like autosuggestions as you type:

brew install zsh-autosuggestions
echo "source $(brew --prefix)/share/zsh-autosuggestions/zsh-autosuggestions.zsh" >> ~/.zshrc

docs.title: zsh-syntax-highlighting
docs.text:
Real-time command validation with color:

brew install zsh-syntax-highlighting
echo "source $(brew --prefix)/share/zsh-syntax-highlighting/zsh-syntax-highlighting.zsh" >> ~/.zshrc

docs.title: tmux
docs.text:
Start a new named session:

tmux new -s work

Detach from a session:

Ctrl+B then D

List sessions:

tmux ls

Reattach to a session:

tmux attach -t work

docs.title: Common Key Bindings
docs.text:
All prefixed with Ctrl+B:

Keys Action " Split pane horizontally % Split pane vertically Arrow keys Switch between panes c Create new window n / p Next / previous window z Toggle pane zoom (fullscreen) d Detach session

docs.title: Useful Shell Functions
docs.text: Add to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc:

docs.title: mkcd — create and enter a directory
docs.text: mkcd() { mkdir -p "$1" && cd "$1"; }

docs.title: extract — universal archive extractor
docs.text:
extract() {
  case "$1" in
    *.tar.bz2) tar xjf "$1" ;;
    *.tar.gz)  tar xzf "$1" ;;
    *.tar.xz)  tar xJf "$1" ;;
    *.zip)     unzip "$1" ;;
    *.gz)      gunzip "$1" ;;
    *.7z)      7z x "$1" ;;
    *)         echo "unsupported: $1" ;;
  esac
}

docs.title: Dotfile Management
docs.text:
Track your config files with a bare git repo:

git init --bare $HOME/.dotfiles
alias dotfiles='git --git-dir=$HOME/.dotfiles --work-tree=$HOME'
dotfiles config --local status.showUntrackedFiles no

Add files:

dotfiles add ~/.zshrc ~/.gitconfig
dotfiles commit -m "add shell config"
dotfiles remote add origin git@github.com:user/dotfiles.git
dotfiles push -u origin main
